bodyTemperature.name: Body Temperature Calculator
bodyTemperature.description: Convert body temperature between °C and °F and interpret fever, normal, and hypothermia ranges.

meta.title: Body Temperature Calculator — Fever, Normal & Hypothermia Ranges
meta.description: Convert body temperature between Celsius and Fahrenheit and instantly see whether your reading indicates normal, fever, or hypothermia. Free online tool.

bodyTemperature.title: Body Temperature Calculator
bodyTemperature.description: Enter your body temperature to convert between °C and °F and get an instant interpretation — from hypothermia to high fever.
bodyTemperature.disclaimer: This calculator is for informational purposes only and does not constitute medical advice. If you are concerned about a fever or low body temperature, consult a healthcare professional.

categoryDesc.hypothermia: Core body temperature is dangerously low (below 35 °C / 95 °F). Seek emergency medical care immediately.
categoryDesc.low: Slightly below the normal range. May occur with cold exposure or illness. Monitor closely.
categoryDesc.normal: Temperature is within the healthy range (36.1–37.2 °C / 97–99 °F). No action needed.
categoryDesc.elevated: Slightly above normal. This may be caused by mild physical activity, stress, or the early stages of illness.
categoryDesc.fever: Fever confirmed (38.1–39 °C / 100.6–102.2 °F). Rest and stay hydrated. Consult a doctor if it persists.
categoryDesc.high_fever: High fever (39.1–40 °C / 102.4–104 °F). Medical attention is recommended, especially for children.
categoryDesc.very_high_fever: Very high fever (above 40 °C / 104 °F). Seek medical care promptly — this is potentially dangerous.

info.measurementDesc: Normal temperature varies by measurement site: rectal (36.6–38 °C), oral (35.5–37.5 °C), axillary (34.7–37.3 °C), and tympanic (35.8–38 °C). Rectal measurement is most accurate.
info.variationDesc: Body temperature fluctuates by up to 0.5 °C throughout the day. It is lowest in the early morning and peaks in the late afternoon. Ovulation also raises temperature slightly.
info.feverDesc: Fever above 38 °C (100.4 °F) in adults lasting more than 3 days, or above 39 °C (102.2 °F) at any age, warrants medical evaluation. Any fever in infants under 3 months needs immediate attention.
info.hypothermiaDesc: Hypothermia occurs when body temperature drops below 35 °C (95 °F). Causes include cold exposure, immersion in cold water, and certain medical conditions. It is a medical emergency.
